decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to overwhelm the opponent with aggressive plays and disruption. By utilizing efficient creatures like Hypnotic Specter and Nantuko Shade, along with removal spells like Diabolic Edict and Smother, the deck aims to control the game and pressure the opponent's resources. With discard spells like Cabal Therapy and Duress, the deck can disrupt the opponent's game plan before they can establish a board presence. The inclusion of Mishra's Factory and Wasteland also provides additional utility and disruption options to further disrupt the opponent's strategy.

The main focus of this deck is to aggressively play and destroy lands while quickly building up resources with cards like Gemstone Mine and Mox Diamond. It aims to disrupt the opponent's mana base with cards like Armageddon and Wasteland, while also providing threats like Terravore and Call of the Herd to close out the game quickly. The deck also includes card draw with Sylvan Library and removal options like Swords to Plowshares to maintain board control. Overall, the deck looks to overwhelm the opponent with an aggressive land destruction strategy.

The main concept of this deck is to quickly flood the board with aggressive Goblin creatures, using cards like Goblin Lackey to cheat them into play and Goblin Warchief to reduce their casting cost. Goblin Piledriver and Siege-Gang Commander provide powerful finishers, while cards like Gempalm Incinerator and Goblin Sharpshooter give removal options. The deck also includes some disruption cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port to disrupt the opponent's mana base. Overall, the goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of Goblins and win the game quickly.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly fill the graveyard with powerful creatures using cards like Buried Alive and Cabal Therapy, then bring them back onto the battlefield with cards like Reanimate and Exhume. This allows the deck to take advantage of powerful creatures like Akroma, Angel of Wrath and Verdant Force much earlier than usual. The deck also includes disruption cards like Unmask and Funeral Charm to disrupt opponent's game plan and protect its own combos.

This premodern BW Control deck focuses on disrupting opponent's strategy with hand disruption, removal spells, and land destruction. It aims to control the game by using powerful cards like Vindicate, Swords to Plowshares, and Wrath of God to deal with threats while generating card advantage with cards like Gerrard's Verdict and Skeletal Scrying. The deck also includes options for recursion and token generation to maintain board presence. With a mix of versatile removal spells and resource denial, this deck aims to outvalue opponents and secure victory through attrition.

The main concept of this deck is to use Ill-Gotten Gains in combination with other cards such as Brain Freeze, Cabal Ritual, and Necrologia to create a powerful combo that allows for quick and efficient wins against opponents. The deck focuses on using cards like Lion's Eye Diamond and Lotus Petal to generate mana quickly, while also utilizing cards like Intuition and Cunning Wish to search for key pieces needed for the combo. Overall, the deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's gameplay to secure a victory.

The deck utilizes a combination of cards to quickly put Phyrexian Dreadnought into play while preventing opponents from countering or removing it. Cards like Stifle, Foil, and Misdirection are used to protect the combo, while Gush and Opt provide card draw and filtering. The deck aims to establish a powerful threat early in the game and protect it long enough to secure a victory. Other cards like Counterspell and Daze help control the opponent's plays, ensuring the combo can resolve successfully.

This deck focuses on controlling the game through countering opponent's spells, removing threats with cards like Swords to Plowshares and Wrath of God, and using Standstill to draw cards while preventing opponents from playing more cards. It also utilizes land destruction with cards like Dust Bowl and Mishra's Factory to disrupt opponent's mana base. The deck aims to stall the game and outvalue opponents with card advantage and efficient removal spells, ultimately taking control of the game and winning with powerful finishers like Decree of Justice.

The deck focuses on quickly ramping up mana with cards like Fyndhorn Elves and Priest of Titania to play powerful creatures like Deranged Hermit and Multani's Acolyte. Quirion Ranger and Wirewood Symbiote provide additional mana and card advantage, while Survival of the Fittest allows for tutoring key creatures. Anger and Masticore can clear the board if needed, while Tangle Wire slows down the opponent's plays. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with a swarm of Elves and powerful creatures, utilizing Gaea's Cradle as a key piece for explosive turns.

The main concept of this deck is to overwhelm your opponent with a fast and aggressive Goblin tribal strategy. By utilizing cards such as Goblin Lackey and Goblin Warchief to cheat out creatures and reduce costs, along with powerful attackers like Goblin Piledriver and Siege-Gang Commander, this deck aims to apply early pressure and maintain a strong board presence. Cards like Gempalm Incinerator and Goblin Sharpshooter provide removal options, while Wasteland and Rishadan Port disrupt your opponent's mana base. Overall, the strategy revolves around quickly building a formidable Goblin army and overwhelming your opponent before they have a chance to stabilize.

This deck focuses on controlling the game early with counterspells and removal spells while gradually building up card advantage with draw spells like Accumulated Knowledge and Gush. Quirion Dryad and Psychatog serve as win conditions, growing stronger as the game progresses. Armageddon can disrupt opponent's mana base, while Fire/Ice provides additional removal and card draw options. The deck aims to outvalue and outlast opponents through efficient control spells and powerful late-game threats.

The main concept of this deck revolves around controlling the game through various means such as counter spells, removal spells, and card advantage. By playing powerful control cards like standalone, counterspell, swords to plowshares, and wrath of god, this deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while efficiently managing resources. Additionally, utilizing standstill as a card advantage engine and faerie conclave as a win condition, this control-landstill deck focuses on maintaining board control and slowly grinding out a victory.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and disruptive spells in order to control the game and chip away at the opponent's life total. By using efficient creatures like Hypnotic Specter and Nantuko Shade, along with removal spells like Smother and Diabolic Edict, the deck aims to maintain pressure on the opponent while disrupting their strategy with cards like Cabal Therapy and Duress. The deck also utilizes utility lands like Mishra's Factory and Wasteland to further control the board and keep the opponent on the back foot.

This Mono Black Aggro deck focuses on using efficient black creatures and disruptive spells to quickly overwhelm the opponent. By combining aggressive creatures like Hypnotic Specter and Nantuko Shade with disruptive spells like Cabal Therapy and Duress, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan while applying pressure on the board. Cards like Cursed Scroll and Graveborn Muse provide additional reach and card advantage, making it difficult for the opponent to stabilize. The inclusion of Wasteland also helps to disrupt the opponent's mana base, giving the deck an edge in the early game.

This RDW deck aims to quickly overwhelm opponents with a relentless barrage of aggressive creatures and burn spells. By utilizing efficient creatures like Jackal Pup and Mogg Fanatic, along with powerful burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Fireblast, the deck seeks to deal damage quickly and efficiently. The inclusion of cards like Flame Rift and Price of Progress helps to push through additional damage, while cards like Cursed Scroll and Sulfuric Vortex provide additional sources of damage over time. The deck's streamlined game plan focuses on applying pressure early and closing out the game before opponents can stabilize.

The deck focuses on utilizing a combination of creatures and control elements to achieve a strong board presence and disrupt the opponent's strategy. By utilizing creatures like Basking Rootwalla and Birds of Paradise alongside powerful spells like Opposition and Natural Order, the deck can generate a strong advantage. The inclusion of Survival of the Fittest allows for further flexibility and consistency in finding key creatures. Overall, the deck aims to outvalue and outmaneuver the opponent through a combination of creature synergy and control elements.
